response:
To find the determinant of a 2×2 matrix:

$$
\begin{bmatrix}
a & b \\
c & d
\end{bmatrix}
$$

we use the formula:

$$
\text{det} = ad - bc
$$

Given the matrix:

$$
\begin{bmatrix}
-\frac{13}{5} & -\frac{37}{10} \\
-\frac{39}{10} & \frac{1}{10}
\end{bmatrix}
$$

Let $ a = -\frac{13}{5} $, $ b = -\frac{37}{10} $, $ c = -\frac{39}{10} $, and $ d = \frac{1}{10} $.

Now compute the determinant:

$$
\text{det} = ad - bc
$$

$$
\text{det} = \left(-\frac{13}{5}\right)\left(\frac{1}{10}\right) - \left(-\frac{37}{10}\right)\left(-\frac{39}{10}\right)
$$

$$
\text{det} = -\frac{13}{50} - \frac{1443}{100}
$$

To combine the terms, express them with a common denominator (100):

$$
\text{det} = -\frac{26}{100} - \frac{1443}{100} = -\frac{1469}{100}
$$

### Final Answer:

$$
\boxed{-\frac{1469}{100}}
$$
